---
title: "Buoyant_Weighing_March2026"
author: "Chloé Gilligan"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:


```{r}
library(readr)
library(lme4)
library(car) 
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse) 
library(dplyr)
library(lmerTest)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(dunn.test)
library(emmeans)
```

# Load data
```{r}
tissue <- read_csv("../data/20260319_BuoyantWeighing_Measurements.csv")
```

# Clean + extract treatment info
```{r}
tissue <- tissue %>%
  rename(
    Sample_ID     = `Sample ID`,
    Tissue_Weight = `Tissue weight`
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Tissue_Weight = as.numeric(Tissue_Weight),
    Sample_Code   = str_extract(Sample_ID, "^[A-Z]{3}"),   # e.g. CRB
    Color         = str_sub(Sample_Code, 3, 3),             # 3rd letter: B, G, R, P
    Site          = str_sub(Sample_Code, 1, 2),             # first 2 letters: CR, BR, RI
  )

# Decode treatments 
tissue <- tissue %>%
  mutate(
    Temperature = case_when(
      Color %in% c("B", "G") ~ "Ambient",
      Color %in% c("R", "P") ~ "Warming"
    ),
    Risk = case_when(
      Color %in% c("B", "R") ~ "Present",
      Color %in% c("G", "P") ~ "Absent"
    ),
    Temperature = factor(Temperature, levels = c("Ambient", "Warming")),
    Risk        = factor(Risk,        levels = c("Absent", "Present")),
    Sample_Code = factor(Sample_Code)
  )

```

# Remove bad values
```{r}
tissue <- tissue %>%
  filter(!is.na(Tissue_Weight), Tissue_Weight > 0)
```

#just checking
```{r}
tissue %>% distinct(Sample_Code, Color, Site, Temperature, Risk) %>% arrange(Sample_Code)
```

#Plotting updated
```{r}
pal_temp <- c("Ambient" = "#1C86EE", "Warming" = "#EE0000")

p_tissue <- ggplot(tissue, aes(x = Risk, y = Tissue_Weight, color = Temperature)) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar",
    width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_temp) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Absent" = "No Crab", "Present" = "Crab")) +
  labs(
    x     = "Predation Risk",
    y     = "Tissue Weight (g)",
    color = "Temperature"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_tissue
```
#Basic stats
```{r}
stats <- tissue %>%
  group_by(Temperature, Risk) %>%
  summarise(
    n           = n(),
    mean_tissue = mean(Tissue_Weight, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_tissue   = sd(Tissue_Weight,   na.rm = TRUE),
    se_tissue   = sd_tissue / sqrt(n),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )
print(stats)
```

```{r}
# Levene's test for homogeneity of variance
leveneTest(Tissue_Weight ~ Temperature * Risk, data = tissue)
```

#LMER

```{r}
options(contrasts = c("contr.sum", "contr.poly"))

# Ensure Site and Date are factors before modelling
tissue <- tissue %>%
  mutate(
    Site  = factor(Site),
    Batch = factor(Date)   # Date used as batch
  )

m1_tissue <- lmer(
  Tissue_Weight ~ Temperature * Risk + (1 | Sample_Code),
  data = tissue,
  REML = TRUE
)
```

#Residuals
```{r}
qqnorm(residuals(m1_tissue), main = "QQ Plot - Tissue Weight")
qqline(residuals(m1_tissue), col = "red", lwd = 2)

plot(fitted(m1_tissue), residuals(m1_tissue),
     xlab = "Fitted", ylab = "Residuals",
     main = "Residuals vs Fitted")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
```

#ANOVA
```{r}
Anova(m1_tissue, test = "F", type = "III")
summary(m1_tissue)
```


#post-hoc


```{r}
#Since not significant
emmeans(m1_tissue, ~ Temperature)
emmeans(m1_tissue, ~ Risk)
emmeans(m1_tissue, ~ Temperature * Risk)
```

```{r}
# Raw means 
tissue %>%
  group_by(Temperature, Risk) %>%
  summarise(raw_mean = mean(Tissue_Weight, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Model-adjusted means 
emmeans(m1_tissue, ~ Temperature * Risk)
```
```{r}
# Look at variance components
summary(m1_tissue)
```

```{r}
options(contrasts = c("contr.sum", "contr.poly"))

m1_tissue <- lmer(
  Tissue_Weight ~ Temperature * Risk + Length + Width + (1 | Sample_Code),
  data = tissue,
  REML = TRUE
)

summary(m1_tissue)
Anova(m1_tissue, test = "F", type = "III")
```
